main_claim: Drug X treats condition Y and has absolutely no side effects.

subclaims.label: supported
subclaims.justification: Clinical trials show Drug X effectively treats condition Y.

subclaims.subclaim: Drug X has no side effects.
subclaims.label: refuted
subclaims.justification: Multiple studies document severe side effects including nausea and headaches.

description: AND logic where one critical premise is refuted.

main_claim: Condition Z can be treated with either Drug A or Surgery B.

subclaims.subclaim: Condition Z can be treated with Drug A.
subclaims.label: supported
subclaims.justification: Drug A is a standard first-line treatment for Condition Z.

subclaims.subclaim: Condition Z can be treated with Surgery B.
subclaims.label: refuted
subclaims.justification: Surgery B is contraindicated and never used for Condition Z.

description: OR logic where one pathway is supported, so the overall claim is partially/mostly supported.

main_claim: Vaccine V is highly effective against the virus, but requires a booster after 6 months to maintain protection.

subclaims.subclaim: Vaccine V is highly effective against the virus.
subclaims.label: supported

subclaims.subclaim: Vaccine V requires a booster after 6 months.
subclaims.label: not_enough_information
subclaims.justification: There is no clinical data on the necessity of a booster after 6 months.

description: AND logic where one premise lacks information, leading to NEI for the whole claim.

main_claim: Therapy T improves memory and reduces joint pain in elderly patients.

subclaims.subclaim: Therapy T improves memory in elderly patients.
subclaims.label: supported

subclaims.subclaim: Therapy T reduces joint pain in elderly patients.
subclaims.label: supported

description: AND logic where both premises are supported.
